risk_rationale: The INFORM Consumers Act introduces new requirements for online marketplaces to collect, verify, and disclose information about high-volume third-party sellers, which is not fully covered by the current compliance baseline.

compliance_gaps.baseline_requirement: Business license verification is mandatory for all sellers.
compliance_gaps.regulatory_requirement: Requires information collection and verification of high-volume third-party sellers.
compliance_gaps.gap_description: The baseline requires business license verification for all sellers, but the new regulation specifically targets high-volume sellers and includes additional requirements for consumer disclosure and suspicious activity reporting.

remediations.title: Enhance Seller Verification and Disclosure Processes
remediations.description: Implement processes to collect, verify, and disclose information about high-volume third-party sellers, including establishing a mechanism for reporting suspicious activities.
acceptance_criteria: High-volume sellers are identified and verified according to the new requirements.
Seller information is disclosed to consumers as mandated.
A reporting mechanism for suspicious activities is implemented.

business_impact: Failure to comply with the INFORM Consumers Act could lead to regulatory penalties and damage to consumer trust, impacting the platform's reputation and operations in the US market.
recommended_actions: Review and update seller verification processes to align with the INFORM Consumers Act.
Develop a consumer disclosure feature for high-volume seller information.
Implement a reporting mechanism for suspicious seller activities.
